name: xenv

metadata.name: xenv
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)

documentation:
# xenv: eXtended ENV

xenv consists in classes and functions to manipulate the environment in a
platform independent way.

In particular it allows to describe the changest to the environment in an XML
file that can be used by the script `xenv` (similar to the Unix `env`
command) to run a command in the modified environment.

documentation: Base class for the objects used to process the variables.

Base class for the classes used to manipulate the environment.

classes.name: xenv.Variable.List

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.name
documentation: Returns the name of the List.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.set
documentation: Sets the value of the List. Any previous value is overwritten.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.unset
documentation: Sets the value of the List to empty. Any previous value is overwritten.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.value
documentation: Returns values of the List. Either as a list or string with desired separator.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.remove
documentation: Removes value(s) from List. If value is not found, removal is canceled.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.append
documentation: Adds value(s) at the end of the list.

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.prepend
documentation:
Adds value(s) at the beginning of the list.
resolve references and duplications

methods.name: xenv.Variable.List.search
documentation:
Searches in List's values for a match

Use string value or set regExp to True.
In the first case search is done only for an exact match for one of List`s value ('^' and '$' added).

Class for manipulating with environment lists.

It holds its name and values represented by a list.
Some operations are done with separator, which is usually colon. For windows use semicolon.

Class for manipulating with environment scalars.

classes.name: xenv.Control.Environment

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.__init__
documentation:
Initial variables to be pushed and setup

append switch between append and prepend for initial variables.
loadFromSystem causes variable`s system value to be loaded on first encounter.
If useAsWriter == True than every change to variables is recorded to XML file.
reportLevel sets the level of messaging.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment._locate
documentation: Find 'filename' in the internal search path.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.vars
documentation: returns dictionary of all variables optionally converted to string

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.var
documentation: Gets a single variable. If not available then tries to load from system.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.search
documentation: Searches in a variable for a value.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment._guessType
documentation:
Guess the type of the variable from its name: if the name contains
'PATH' or 'DIRS', then the variable is a list, otherwise it is a scalar.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.declare
documentation: Creates an instance of new variable. It loads values from the OS if the variable is not local.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.append
documentation: Appends to an existing variable.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.prepend
documentation: Prepends to an existing variable, or create a new one.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.set
documentation: Sets a single variable - overrides any previous value!

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.default
documentation: Sets a single variable only if it is not already set!

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.unset
documentation: Unsets a single variable to an empty value - overrides any previous value!

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.remove
documentation: Remove value from variable.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.searchFile
documentation: Searches for appearance of variable in a file.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.loadXML
documentation: Loads XML file for input variables.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.startXMLinput
documentation: Renew writer for new input.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.finishXMLinput
documentation: Finishes input of XML file and closes the file.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.writeToFile
documentation: Creates an output file with a specified name to be used for setting variables by sourcing this file

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.writeToXMLFile
documentation:
Writes the current state of environment to a XML file.

NOTE: There is no trace of actions taken, variables are written with a set action only.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.presetFromSystem
documentation: Loads all variables from the current system settings.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment.process
documentation: Call the variable processors on all the variables.

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment._concatenate
documentation: Returns a variable string with separator separator from the values list

methods.name: xenv.Control.Environment._writeVarToXML
documentation: Writes single variable to XML file.
object to hold settings of environment

classes.name: xenv.Script

methods.name: xenv.Script.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the script instance parsing the command line arguments (or
the explicit arguments provided).

methods.name: xenv.Script._prepare_parser
documentation:
Prepare an OptionParser instance used to analyze the command line
options and arguments.

methods.name: xenv.Script._parse_args
documentation: Parse the command line arguments.

methods.name: xenv.Script._check_args
documentation: Check consistency of command line options and arguments.

methods.name: xenv.Script._makeEnv
documentation:
Generate a dictionary of the environment variables after applying all
the required actions.

methods.name: xenv.Script.dump
documentation: Print to standard output the final environment in the required format.

methods.name: xenv.Script.expandEnvVars
documentation:
Return a copy of iterable where all the elements have the environment
variables expanded.

```pycon
>>> s = Script([])
>>> s.env = {'A': '1', 'B': 'test'}
>>> s.expandEnvVars(['$A', '${B}-$A', '$DUMMY-$A', '$$B'])
['1', 'test-1', '$DUMMY-1', '$B']
```

methods.name: xenv.Script.runCmd
documentation: Execute a command in the modified environment and return the exit code.

methods.name: xenv.Script.main
documentation: Main function of the script.

Environment Script class used to control the logic of the script and allow
extensions.

Context Manager protocol 'exit' function.
Remove the temporary directory and let the exceptions propagate.

Helper class to create a temporary directory and manage its lifetime.

An instance of this class can be used inside the 'with' statement and
returns the path to the temporary directory.
